records.ety: [OE. aouren, anouren, adoren, OF. aorer, adorer, F. adorer, fr. L. adorare; ad + orare to speak, pray, os, oris, mouth. In OE. confused with honor, the French prefix a- being confused with OE. a, an, on. See Oral.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. adored (*dōrd"); p. pr. & vb. n. adoring (*dōr"ng).]

defs: To worship with profound reverence; to pay divine honors to; to honor as a deity or as divine.
quotes: Bishops and priests, . . . bearing the host, which he [James II.] publicly adored.

defs: To love in the highest degree; to regard with the utmost esteem and affection; to idolize.
quotes: The great mass of the population abhorred Popery and adored Monmouth.
Congealed little drops which do the morn adore.
